{"id":"steelmade-e6o","title":"Investigate /chairs visual inconsistency and Sanity health","description":"Analyze why https://steelmade.co.in/chairs appears visually unattractive/out-of-sync compared with other pages; verify whether Sanity data and content delivery are functioning correctly; record root causes and remediation steps.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"dev@localhost","created_at":"2026-03-04T18:18:49Z","created_by":"Automated Committer","updated_at":"2026-03-04T18:18:49Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Findings (2026-03-04):\\n1) Sanity health check: PASS. Using existing .env.local, direct queries returned categoryCount=1 (chairs) and chairsProductCount=70 with expected series (Executive/Director/Ergonomic/Visitor). This indicates CMS data is available and queryable.\\n2) Live route behavior: /chairs and /desks currently return valid CSS+JS assets (200).\\n3) Critical inconsistency detected: / (homepage) HTML references stale CSS/JS chunk hashes that return 404. Evidence: /_next/static/css/85b6477f8d50d2cb.css -\u003e 404, and multiple /_next/static/chunks/* from / return 404.\\n4) Cache-age mismatch confirms stale HTML at edge: / response age ~350k seconds while /chairs age ~130 seconds. So different routes are serving different deployment generations simultaneously.\\n5) PWA/service-worker is active (sw.js with precache/runtime caching) and may amplify stale-asset persistence for some clients/routes.\\n6) Likely root cause of unstyled/ugly/out-of-sync experiences: stale HTML + dead asset hashes from mixed deploy caches (plus SW cache), not Sanity content failure.\\n7) Secondary UX cause: category template design is intentionally minimal and visually different from homepage, which can feel inconsistent even when technically working.\\nRecommended remediations:\\n- Remove timestamp-based generateBuildId (Date.now) to avoid unstable build identity per deploy.\\n- Trigger clean Netlify deploy with cache clear and invalidate stale edge HTML.\\n- Temporarily disable or carefully version PWA caching until asset consistency is stable.\\n- Align category page visual system (typography/spacing/hero controls) with homepage components for brand consistency."}
